body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Closes #1190 

This PR makes all experiments and docs use the `LandSimulation` struct. This also means that all changed simulations will use ITime internally. 


## Content
Add `LandSimulation` constructors that handle ITime conversion.

Make callbacks work with `ITime`

Add kwargs to `LandSimulation`

Drop `FT` as argument to `LandSimulation`

If an experiment/simulation has a start_date anywhere, use start and end date instead of t0 and tf. This makes it so that each simulation only has one notion of time.

TODO:

Some of the output plots do not match because the callback saving order is modified. Fix this before merging. Examples are ozark_soilsnow and snowmip.

#1309 clean up the `set_ic!` functions

- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

title: Tr/use land simulation struct

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
This followup PR to #1223 implements the "subdaily P-model", which explicitly models both the slow (weekly) timescale optimal acclimatization of photosynthetic capacities (Vcmax25, Jmax25) and sensitivity of stomatal closure to dryness ($\xi$), as well as the fast (instantaneous) timescale adjustment of these capacities to temperature (for Vcmax and Jmax) and VPD (for computing $c_i$ from $\xi$). Closes #1206. 

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->
- We need a way of ensuring that the P-model callback is included in the callbacks when we run a simulation using the P-model. The model will not work if the callback is not included, but currently we don't check for this
- Implement soil moisture stress as a modular parameterization (PR forthcoming) 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
We follow the scheme introduced in Mengoli et al. (2022), who propose computing optimal parameters at local noon and modeling weekly acclimatization using an exponential moving average. The implementation details are as follows:
- Local noon is computed via the longitude of the domain and represented as seconds UTC. Longitude can be passed in directly as a float, or automatically inferred from the canopy_domain, if it is provided. 
- We do not account for the [equation of time](https://clima.github.io/Insolation.jl/dev/ZenithAngleEquations/#Equation-of-Time) correction to compute local noon, so it should not be interpreted literally as local noon. This introduces an error of at most ~20 minutes, which is unconsequential for the purpose of computing optimal photosynthetic parameters (see Mengoli et al. 2022). As such, local noon is constant throughout the year and solely dependent on longitude.
- We create a new callback `frequency_based_callback` which checks for local noon every simulation timestep `dt` and updates the optimal parameters if $t \in [t_{noon} - dt/2, t_{noon} + dt/2]$
- Because the exponential moving average requires the last computed value, we add new variables to the cache as a NamedTuple `p.canopy.photosynthesis.OptVars` to store these variables. We use a NamedTuple because of shared computation. 
- Use of past values requires some special logic at initialization. Two possible schemes are 1) computing optimal parameters using initial conditions $(Y_0, p_0)$; 2) use pre-set optimal parameters e.g., from a spun-up long run or a map of parameters. Currently we implement option (1). This means that it takes ~1 month of spinup time for the optimal photosynthetic parameters to acclimate to realistic values. 
- Since the P-model also predicts stomatal conductance $g_s$, a `PModelConductance` model is added to `stomatal_conductance.jl`. When constructing a `canopy`, we check that if we use P-model for photosynthesis, we should also be using P-model conductance for physical consistency. 


### Citation
Mengoli, G., Agustí-Panareda, A., Boussetta, S., Harrison, S. P., Trotta, C., & Prentice, I. C. (2022). Ecosystem photosynthesis in land-surface models: A first-principles approach incorporating acclimation. Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems, 14, e2021MS002767. https://doi.org/10.1029/2021MS002767

title: Implement subdaily P-model

## Purpose
Improve documentation for fluxnet and global runs; simplify table of contents in docs
preview: https://clima.github.io/ClimaLand.jl/previews/PR1288/

Bucket Plots:
[bucket_figures.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21759022/bucket_figures.pdf)
[bucket_annual_timeseries.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21759023/bucket_annual_timeseries.pdf)

Snow land fluxnet:
[US_NR1_variable_timeseries.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21759185/US_NR1_variable_timeseries.pdf)
[US_NR1_diurnal_timeseries.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21759186/US_NR1_diurnal_timeseries.pdf)

Soil canopy
[US_MOz_diurnal_timeseries.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21760378/US_MOz_diurnal_timeseries.pdf)


## To-do

Future PRs
**- Change default plotting to png - otherwise we cannot show pdf in doc markdown**
## Content
docs: 
- Rearrange docs a bit: create a fluxnet simulation group, and a global sim group, in addition to standalone model group
- add fluxnet documentation (soil canopy and snowy land runs, visualization, forcing data)
- add global run documentation (bucket + snowy land)
- added stand in pages for things we know we need to add
To review: @juliasloan25  all docs/ changes and src/integrated/soil_canopy_model.jl
@AlexisRenchon all docs/ changes and src/integrated/soil_canopy_model.jl

fluxnet and simulations:
- Add make_set_fluxnet_ic function to hide closure from user and be more in line with how we create the ic function in the global case
- added start_date to Simulation struct (used in plotting fluxnet runs)
- small other changes
- @imreddyTeja could review: - ozark_pft.jl
- ozark_soilsnow.jl
- run_fluxnet.jl
- ozark_conservation.jl
- ext/FluxnetSimulationsExt.jl
- ext/LandSimulationVisualizationExt.jl
- ext/fluxnet_simulations/initial_conditions.jl
 - ext/land_sim_vis/plotting_utils.jl
- src/simulations/Simulations.jl

Moved to PR 1315:
- src/standalone/Vegetation/Canopy.jl
- test/integrated/full_land.jl
-test/integrated/full_land_utils.jl
- test/standalone/Soil/Biogeochemistry/biogeochemistry_module.jl
-
